behaviors.description: Reject request with mismatched `X-Signature-256` as 401 and do not forward body.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler + signature verifier + queue handoff. A pure unit test can miss wiring bugs (e.g., verifier result ignored, enqueue still called). Integration is sufficient with in-process HTTP app and a real/raw-body parsing path plus spy/fake queue. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; in-process request execution can reproduce the failure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Start the HTTP handler in-process, send a request with intentionally incorrect HMAC over the raw body, assert `401`, and assert queue `enqueue` was never invoked and no persisted record exists.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test uses module interaction (HTTP handler + verifier + queue boundary), which is exactly Integration.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200 acknowledgment.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across modules (webhook handler and persistent queue write). Minimum real failure is at state handoff/transaction boundary, not single-function logic. Integration with real queue module (local persistence) can catch ack-before-write regressions. Rejected System: behavior text does not require external networked queue or subprocess to surface ordering bug. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument queue write completion (or commit hook), issue valid webhook request, and assert the persistence write completes before response is observed as `200` (e.g., record timestamp/order or latch sequencing).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Ordering guarantee is cross-module; test requires integrated handler+queue wiring.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing completion.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in orchestration between request path and async processor. Integration is needed to prove handler decouples ack from processing future/promise. Rejected System: no required real OS/network primitive beyond app modules; delayed in-process downstream worker is enough. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to a downstream processor that blocks on a controllable promise; send valid request; assert response returns immediately (before releasing processor promise), then release promise and verify processing eventually completes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Demonstrates async decoupling at module boundary, which matches Integration.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3s wall-clock under concurrent slow downstream processing of earlier events.
behaviors.justification: This is a real-time concurrency SLO tied to wall-clock behavior under load. In-process mocks/fake timers can hide scheduler/event-loop starvation and actual server concurrency effects. Requires running real server timing and concurrent requests to catch real failure. Rejected Integration: behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock and concurrent runtime characteristics beyond simple module wiring. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service process with real clock, pre-load slow downstream work (or earlier events still processing), send concurrent valid webhook(s), and assert each HTTP ack latency is <3s measured externally end-to-end.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Latency under concurrent runtime pressure needs system-level execution, not isolated module tests.

behaviors.description: Duplicate event ID is not written to queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at handler-to-persistent-queue boundary (idempotency check + write path). Unit tests can miss uniqueness wiring/transaction behavior. Integration with real persistence layer catches real duplicate-write failures. Rejected System: no explicit need for real network/OS/subprocess; local persistence integration is enough. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same valid event ID twice through the handler; assert first returns success and one queue record exists, second does not create an additional record (and verify write-count remains 1).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Cross-module idempotency is exercised with real persistence integration.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, handler returns 503 with `Retry-After`.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is mapping of queue backpressure signal to HTTP response semantics. Requires handler + queue interaction, not just local function logic. Integration is sufficient with queue configured at capacity. Rejected System: no required real external infra; local module wiring can reproduce. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Fill queue to configured capacity, submit another valid webhook, assert `503` and presence/value of `Retry-After`, and assert no extra queue write occurred.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Validates contract across queue and HTTP layers, fitting Integration.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow events.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is inside queue module plus real local persistence semantics (capacity accounting across writes). A pure unit fake can miss persistence-backed enforcement bugs. Integration with actual queue storage is minimum. Rejected System: behavior text does not require subprocess/real network/external API. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize queue with small capacity in test persistence backend, enqueue up to limit successfully, then enqueue one more and assert explicit rejection/error and unchanged persisted count.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real queue+storage wiring; no higher-level infrastructure required.
